mnemonics: F.O.R.C.E. (Factors of a Business Case): Feasibility, Options, Risks, Costs, Expected Benefits
keyPoints: A business case justifies the project by analyzing costs, benefits, risks, and alternatives.
It is a key decision-making document for project initiation and funding.
It must clearly articulate the problem being solved and the value proposition of the proposed solution.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on technical benefits and ignoring tangible financial or strategic business value.
Underestimating costs (e.g., maintenance, training) and overestimating benefits.
Failing to analyze a 'do nothing' or alternative solutions scenario.
